(née **Wikström**; 12 February 1945) is a Swedish actress and model, best known for her roles as two different [Bond girls](/wiki/Bond_girl "Bond girl"), first in *[The Man with the Golden Gun](/wiki/The_Man_with_the_Golden_Gun_(film) "The Man with the Golden Gun (film)")* (1974) and then as the title character in *[Octopussy](/wiki/Octopussy "Octopussy")* (1983).

Sheff, David. ["Ma Barker and Pal Turn Out to Be Actress Maud Adams and Live-Out Boyfriend Reid Smith"](http://www.people.com/people/archive/article/0,,20076458,00.html), *People*, Vol. 13, No. 19, 12 May 1980.

["The Man With The Golden Gun Almost Ended James Bond Movies"](https://screenrant.com/james-bond-movies-man-golden-gun-bad/). *[Screen Rant](/wiki/Screen_Rant "Screen Rant")*. 22 March 2020. Retrieved 28 April 2020.

["James Bond Mystery SOLVED: Maud Adams Found in *A View To A Kill* ·"](https://web.archive.org/web/20111106132500/http://commanderbond.net/2410/james-bond-mystery-solved-maud-adams-found-in-a-view-to-a-kill.html). Commanderbond.net. 9 June 2004. Archived from [the original](http://commanderbond.net/2410/james-bond-mystery-solved-maud-adams-found-in-a-view-to-a-kill.html) on 6 November 2011. Retrieved 27 November 2012.

["Talking to Bond Girls"](https://web.archive.org/web/20110716160525/http://stumpedmagazine.com/Articles/bond-girls-article.html). Stumpedmagazine.com. Archived from [the original](http://stumpedmagazine.com/Articles/bond-girls-article.html) on 16 July 2011. Retrieved 2 August 2010.

From Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia

Swedish actress (born 1945)

This article is about the Swedish actress and model. For the American stage actress, see [Maude Adams](/wiki/Maude_Adams "Maude Adams").

| Maud Adams | |
| --- | --- |
| Adams in 2019 | |
| Born | Maud Solveig Christina Wikström   (1945-02-12) 12 February 1945 (age 81)  [Luleå](/wiki/Lule%C3%A5 "Luleå"), Sweden |
| Occupations | * Actress * model |
| Years active | 1970–present (occasionally since 2010) |
| Spouses | * Roy Adams  ​  ​ (m. 1966; div. 1975)​ * Charles Rubin  ​ (m. 1999)​ |

**Maud Solveig Christina Adams** (née **Wikström**; 12 February 1945) is a Swedish actress and model, best known for her roles as two different [Bond girls](/wiki/Bond_girl "Bond girl"), first in *[The Man with the Golden Gun](/wiki/The_Man_with_the_Golden_Gun_(film) "The Man with the Golden Gun (film)")* (1974) and then as the title character in *[Octopussy](/wiki/Octopussy "Octopussy")* (1983).

She made many other appearances in both films and television including *[The Christian Licorice Store](/wiki/The_Christian_Licorice_Store "The Christian Licorice Store")* (1971), *[Rollerball](/wiki/Rollerball_(1975_film) "Rollerball (1975 film)")* (1975), *[Killer Force](/wiki/Killer_Force "Killer Force")* (1976), *[Merciless Man](/wiki/Merciless_Man "Merciless Man")* (1976), *[Hell Hunters](/wiki/Hell_Hunters "Hell Hunters")* (1986) and *[The Kill Reflex](/wiki/The_Kill_Reflex "The Kill Reflex")* (1989).

## Early life

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Maud_Adams&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Early life")]

Adams was born as Maud Solveig Christina Wikström in [Luleå](/wiki/Lule%C3%A5 "Luleå"), Sweden, the daughter of Thyra, a government tax inspector, and Gustav Wikström, a [comptroller](/wiki/Comptroller "Comptroller").[[1]](#cite_note-1)[*[better source needed](/wiki/Wikipedia:Verifiability#Questionable_sources "Wikipedia:Verifiability")*] She is fluent in five languages, and at one time wanted to work as an interpreter.[[2]](#cite_note-MaBarker-2)

Adams was discovered in 1963 in a shop by a photographer who asked to take her picture, which he then submitted to the *[Miss Sweden](/wiki/Miss_Sweden "Miss Sweden")* contest arranged by the magazine *[Allers](/wiki/Allers_(magazine) "Allers (magazine)")*; from there her modeling career took off.

## Career

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Maud_Adams&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Career")]

Adams moved to Paris and later to New York City to work for [Eileen Ford](/wiki/Eileen_Ford "Eileen Ford"). Her acting career started when she was asked to appear in the 1970 movie *[The Boys in the Band](/wiki/The_Boys_in_the_Band_(1970_film) "The Boys in the Band (1970 film)")*, in which she played a photo-shoot model in the opening credits. During the 1970s she guest-starred in such American TV series as *[Hawaii Five-O](/wiki/Hawaii_Five-O_(1968_TV_series) "Hawaii Five-O (1968 TV series)")* and *[Kojak](/wiki/Kojak "Kojak")*.

Adams was catapulted to international fame when she appeared as the mistress of the villain [Franciso Scaramanga](/wiki/Francisco_Scaramanga "Francisco Scaramanga") in the 1974 [James Bond](/wiki/James_Bond "James Bond") film *[The Man with the Golden Gun](/wiki/The_Man_with_the_Golden_Gun_(film) "The Man with the Golden Gun (film)")*.[[3]](#cite_note-3) In short order she appeared in [Norman Jewison](/wiki/Norman_Jewison "Norman Jewison")'s futuristic *[Rollerball](/wiki/Rollerball_(1975_film) "Rollerball (1975 film)")* (1975) and several European films, and in 1981 she starred in the thriller *[Tattoo](/wiki/Tattoo_(1981_film) "Tattoo (1981 film)")*. She was so well regarded by [Albert Broccoli](/wiki/Albert_Broccoli "Albert Broccoli"), the producer of the [James Bond films](/wiki/James_Bond_film_series "James Bond film series"), that she was asked to return to the series in 1983 for *[Octopussy](/wiki/Octopussy_(film) "Octopussy (film)")*. In this film, she plays the mysterious smuggler Octopussy, again opposite [Roger Moore](/wiki/Roger_Moore "Roger Moore").[[4]](#cite_note-4) Looking back on her two roles in Bond films, Adams said, "How can you not really enjoy the fact that you were a Bond Girl? It's pop culture and to be part of that is very nice."[[5]](#cite_note-5)

She also was the president of a cosmetics company called Scandinavian Biocosmetics.[[6]](#cite_note-6)

* *From Rollerball to Rome* (2020)
* *For Our Eyes Only: John Glenn* (2021)
